skills.name: aoa-adr-write
skills.description: Capture a meaningful architectural or workflow decision as an ADR or decision note, place it in the canonical decision surface, and verify the rationale stays discoverable. Use when structure, boundaries, tooling, or workflow expectations change and future contributors need to know why. Do not use for tiny self-evident edits or when the real problem is source-of-truth ambiguity rather than decision rationale.
should_trigger: Capture a meaningful architectural or workflow decision as an ADR or decision note, place it in the canonical decision surface, and verify the rationale stays discoverable. Use when structure, boundaries, tooling, or workflow expectations change and future contributors need to know why.
negative_controls: The change is tiny and self-evident, with no durable decision or rationale that future contributors would need to find.
notes: Use aoa-adr-write when the task is to capture a concrete architectural or workflow decision.
Use aoa-source-of-truth-check when the task is to identify which documents are authoritative and reduce overlap.

skills.name: aoa-approval-gate-check
skills.description: Classify whether a requested action is safe to proceed, requires explicit approval, or should not be executed. Use when a task may be destructive, security-sensitive, or operationally sensitive and the authority boundary is unclear. Do not use for clearly low-risk work or when the main need is previewing a mutation rather than deciding whether it may proceed.
manual_invocation_required: Classify whether a requested action is safe to proceed, requires explicit approval, or should not be executed. Use when a task may be destructive, security-sensitive, or operationally sensitive and the authority boundary is unclear.
Before touching production DNS records, classify whether this request is safe, approval-gated, or out of bounds.
negative_controls: The task is clearly low-risk, already bounded, and does not look destructive, security-sensitive, or operationally sensitive.
notes: aoa-approval-gate-check classifies risk and whether execution is allowed at all.
aoa-dry-run-first insists on preview or simulation before a real mutation.
aoa-safe-infra-change handles bounded infra or config changes with rollback thinking.
aoa-local-stack-bringup is the local multi-service stack start path and is explicit-only.
aoa-sanitized-share is about turning raw technical material into a safe public artifact.

skills.name: aoa-automation-opportunity-scan
skills.description: Detect reviewed or repeated project processes that are candidates for automation, classify whether they are seed-ready, and route them to the right owner layer without granting schedule or mutation authority. Use when a reviewed session or repeated project slice reveals a recurring manual route and the honest question is whether it should stay manual, become a bounded skill, or become a playbook automation seed candidate. Do not use for one-off exploratory work, creative synthesis with no stable trigger or output, live scheduling requests, or hidden self-change.
manual_invocation_required: Classify whether a recurring reviewed project route is automation-ready, checkpoint-required, or not ready yet without granting scheduler authority, hidden background execution, or silent self-change. Use when the same audit, hygiene pass, report loop, or repair ritual keeps returning and the real question is whether it should remain manual or lift into a bounded automation seed.

This reviewed workflow keeps recurring across sessions with the same inputs, the same approval seam, and the same expected output. Classify whether it should stay manual, become a bounded skill, or become a playbook automation seed with checkpoint requirements called out explicitly.
negative_controls: This only happened once, still looks exploratory, and there is no stable trigger, checkpoint boundary, or repeatable output contract yet, so calling it automation-ready would be premature.
This timeout incident happened once, the evidence is still thin, and there is no stable recurring route or output contract yet. Do not upgrade it into an automation or harvest workflow.

aoa-session-self-diagnose turns repeated friction, drift, or proof blur from reviewed evidence into a diagnosis packet before mutation.
aoa-session-self-repair turns an existing reviewed diagnosis into the smallest honest repair packet with checkpoint posture and rollback thinking.
aoa-session-progression-lift reflects reviewed mastery movement as a bounded multi-axis delta without pretending to be routing or role authority.
aoa-quest-harvest stays the narrow final promotion verdict once the material is already reduced to one repeated reviewed quest unit.

skills.name: aoa-session-self-diagnose
skills.description: Classify drift, friction, proof gaps, ownership confusion, and repeated failure patterns from a reviewed session into a bounded diagnosis packet without mutating anything yet. Use when the next honest move is diagnosis before repair and the reviewed material points to repeated contradictions, blockers, or boundary blur. Do not use for live sessions, for issues that are already fully diagnosed, or when the real task is final quest-promotion triage.
manual_invocation_required: Classify drift, friction, proof gaps, ownership confusion, and repeated failure patterns from a reviewed session into a bounded diagnosis packet without mutating anything yet. Use when repeated contradictions, blockers, or boundary blur suggest diagnosis should happen before repair.
Across the reviewed session notes, the same blockers, proof gaps, and boundary blur keep recurring. Turn that evidence into a diagnosis packet before anyone starts patching.
negative_controls: The session contains no meaningful friction or contradiction, and the next move is straightforward execution rather than diagnosis.
notes: aoa-session-donor-harvest is the post-session nucleus: it authors a bounded HARVEST_PACKET and names the next honest family handoff.

skills.name: aoa-source-of-truth-check
skills.description: Clarify which files are authoritative for repository guidance, operational instructions, architecture, and status, and keep entrypoint docs short and link-driven once canonical homes exist. Use when docs overlap, conflict, or confuse contributors about which file to trust. Do not use for purely code-local tasks or when authoritative files are already clear and the main need is decision rationale.
should_trigger: Clarify which files are authoritative for repository guidance, operational instructions, architecture, and status, and keep entrypoint docs short and link-driven once canonical homes exist. Use when docs overlap, conflict, or confuse contributors about which file to trust.
negative_controls: The repository is tiny and there is no meaningful ambiguity about which file is authoritative.
This repo has three conflicting architecture guides and two outdated runbooks. Identify which files are authoritative and shorten the entrypoints into a link-driven map.

This abyss repo now has a diagnostic spine note, runbook fragments, and overlay guidance that disagree about which file is authoritative. Map the canonical docs and shorten the entrypoints instead of producing a runtime artifact.
notes: Use a project overlay only when the repository actually matches that overlay family and the repo-relative adaptation matters.
Current live overlay families here are the atm10-* and abyss-* repo-local exemplars.
Use the base AoA skill when the workflow does not need a thin project overlay.

skills.name: aoa-summon
skills.description: Delegate one bounded child route through quest-passport law, local-first Codex execution defaults, hard gates for progression, self-agent, and stress posture, and governed return into reviewed closeout. Use when a parent route already has a real anchor and named outputs and the honest next move is a narrower child reviewer, evaluator, verifier, or leaf helper rather than a larger orchestration layer. Do not use when the route lacks an anchor, the outputs are unnamed, the work is `d3+` and still needs a split, or remote delegation is being used to bypass proof, approval, or closeout posture.
manual_invocation_required: Delegate one bounded child route through quest-passport law, local-first Codex execution defaults, hard gates for progression, self-agent, and stress posture, and governed return into reviewed closeout. Use when a parent route already has a real anchor and named outputs and the honest next move is a narrower child reviewer, evaluator, verifier, or leaf helper rather than a larger orchestration layer.

The parent route already has a quest passport, a concrete anchor, and named outputs, and it needs one bounded local reviewer child to inspect a narrow package fix before the parent continues. Decide the honest child lane, gates, and return plan.
negative_controls: There is no real anchor, no named outputs, and the work is still a broad d3-plus route, so pretending one child summon would clarify it would only hide the missing split.
notes: aoa-summon decides whether one anchored parent route may launch a bounded child helper and what lane, gates, and return posture apply.

aoa-change-protocol is about doing the bounded work directly instead of selecting a child route to do it.

skills.name: aoa-tdd-slice
skills.description: Implement a bounded feature slice through test-first discipline, minimal implementation, and explicit refactor boundaries. Use when a behavior change can be specified in tests before code and confidence or regression resistance matters. Do not use for undefined exploratory work, one-off glue, or broader architectural restructuring.
should_trigger: Implement a bounded feature slice through test-first discipline, minimal implementation, and explicit refactor boundaries. Use when a behavior change can be specified in tests before code and confidence or regression resistance matters.
negative_controls: The work is still exploratory and the behavior is not defined enough to drive implementation from tests first.
Add the new password-reset behavior test-first: define the expected flow in tests, implement the smallest passing slice, and keep the refactor bounded.
notes: aoa-change-protocol is the generic bounded-change workflow.
aoa-commit-growth-seam is the narrower post-verify commit boundary when the coding work is already done.
aoa-tdd-slice is specifically test-first feature slicing.
aoa-contract-test is specifically about validating a module/service/workflow boundary.
